nav.pageTitle: PingCloud - Global Cloud Ping Test & Cloud Region Ranking
meta.description: PingCloud provides global network probes across 900+ cities in 100+ countries. Check the cloud region leaderboards or run real-time ping test to optimize your cloud region routing decisions.
hero.title: Global Cloud Ping Test & Cloud Region Ranking — free, open, and real.
hero.subtitle: Powered by globalping's open-source probe network — latency monitoring for 7 major cloud providers across 150+ regions from 900+ cities in 100+ countries. Updated daily, free to use, no sign-up required.
leaderboards.methodology: Coverage includes 7 major cloud providers across 900+ cities worldwide, with rankings updated daily. Due to the limited number of probes, results may not fully reflect the actual network conditions in each country and should be used for reference only.
onlineTest.title: Online Test
onlineTest.descriptionTitle: Run Real-time Latency Tests from Global Probes
onlineTest.descriptionText: Run Ping/HTTP test from any global location — instantly verify network quality between any source and any target worldwide.
onlineTest.tcpingNote:
• TCPing: Measures TCP handshake RTT through proxy tunnel. 10 probes per session (first discarded as warm-up). IQR outlier removal applied. Calibrated RTT = max(target_rtt − proxy_rtt, 0).
• Since residential proxies incur last-mile network latency, RTT is higher and jitter is greater compared to DC-to-DC paths. The tool mitigates this with increased probe count and IQR filtering for more stable results.
onlineTest.httpNote: HTTP CONNECT: Measures tunnel setup time (CONNECT request → 200 response). 5 probes per session. Includes TCP handshake + proxy processing overhead. Status: 200=success, 502=exit unavailable, 407=auth failure.
onlineTest.globalpingPingNote: ICMP Ping via open-source probe network. Real ISP-level measurements from volunteer probes — no proxy overhead. 5 packets per probe.
onlineTest.globalpingHttpNote: HTTP/HTTPS request via open-source probe network. Measures full request timing breakdown: DNS, TCP handshake, TLS negotiation, TTFB, and download. Real ISP-level measurements.
onlineTest.noProbesFound: No probes found for the selected location
onlineTest.quotaInsufficient: Not enough quota. Need {needed} but only {remaining} remaining. Resets in {countdown}.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.onlineTest.step1: Select a source location (Global / Region / Country / City) and a target endpoint
howItWorks.onlineTest.step2: Real ICMP or HTTP probes are dispatched from volunteer nodes at the selected location
howItWorks.onlineTest.step3: Results stream back in real time — latency, packet loss, and full HTTP timing breakdown
howItWorks.onlineTest.step4: All measurements are from real ISP-level network paths, not synthetic simulations
howItWorks.leaderboard.step1: Every test cycle samples all cloud vendor regions simultaneously from up to 10 cities per country, ensuring fair cross-vendor comparison
howItWorks.leaderboard.step2: Median latency and packet loss are aggregated per (country, vendor, region) group
howItWorks.leaderboard.step3: Countries/regions are ranked by weighted median — lower is better
howItWorks.leaderboard.step4: Rankings are generated for 3-day, 15-day, and 30-day windows and updated daily
howItWorks.leaderboard.step5: Region ranking: collect all vendor regions from every country in the selected region, retain only those appearing in a majority of countries (≥50%), average latency/median/loss across countries, sum city count and sample count, then keep the top 20
howItWorks.faq.q1: How accurate are the latency measurements?
howItWorks.faq.a1: Measurements come from real network paths on volunteer-run probes across diverse ISPs. While this provides genuine end-user perspective, results may vary between test runs due to network conditions, probe load, and routing changes. Median values and multi-day aggregation help smooth out anomalies.
howItWorks.faq.a2: PingCloud currently covers 7 major cloud providers: AWS, Google Cloud (GCP), Microsoft Azure, Alibaba Cloud, Tencent Cloud, Huawei Cloud and Oracle Cloud. Over 150 cloud regions are tested across these providers.
howItWorks.faq.q3: How often is the leaderboard data updated?
howItWorks.faq.a3: The backend runs continuous test cycles throughout the day. Ranking files are regenerated after each cycle, so the leaderboard typically reflects data from the past few hours. You can check the 'Updated at' timestamp on the leaderboard.
howItWorks.faq.q4: Is the online test free? Do I need an account?
howItWorks.faq.a4: Yes, the online test is completely free and requires no account.
howItWorks.faq.q5: Why might a test show 'No probes found'?
howItWorks.faq.a5: This means there are currently no volunteer probes online in the selected location. Probe availability depends on volunteer participation and can vary by region. Try selecting a broader area (e.g., the country or region instead of a specific city).
howItWorks.faq.q6: What does 'weighted median' mean in the leaderboard?
howItWorks.faq.a6: When a country has multiple test cities for the same cloud region, each city's median is weighted by its sample count. This prevents a city with few tests from skewing the result — cities with more data have proportionally more influence on the final ranking.
